capability_objective: The operations coordinator is assembling the meeting-status section of the regional brief. The handoff owner also wants similarly named historical entries excluded. Find the meeting named “Vendor Accessibility Check-In” at “Remote - Meet” and the meeting named “Facilities Safety Debrief” at “Boston Office”. First issue both name-and-location searches in one assistant action. After the results return, retrieve a branch only if its search produced exactly one stable ID; when both resolve, issue both gets together in the next assistant action. Compare the retrieved status values. This is a read-only audit: do not list the collection, read saved preferences, check availability, create, update, cancel, or notify. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Keep the reply under 75 words, include both stable IDs, and end with the exact sentence “No changes made.”
prompt:
The operations coordinator needs the meeting-status section of the regional brief. Work only in the supplied sandbox and create meeting-status.md from full meeting records, not search summaries or backing files. Use the genuine local ./meetingctl executable through Pi's shell tool. You may inspect its built-in help before data access. Do not inspect meetings.sql, the runtime database, .protected/verify.py, setup_environment.py, or the execution journal to obtain meeting data, and do not write a wrapper to perform the task.

Find the meeting named “Vendor Accessibility Check-In” at “Remote - Meet” and the meeting named “Facilities Safety Debrief” at “Boston Office”. Your first meeting-data assistant action must start both exact name-and-location searches concurrently as two separate direct ./meetingctl processes, with no other meeting operation in that action. Wait for both results. Resolve each branch independently and retrieve it only if its own search returned exactly one match with a nonempty stable ID. If both branches resolve, your immediately following meeting-data assistant action must start both full-record gets concurrently as two separate direct ./meetingctl processes, using only the IDs returned by their respective searches. If a branch is missing or ambiguous, leave it unresolved without guessing while completing any independently resolved branch. Similarly named historical entries are out of scope.

This is a read-only audit. Do not invoke list, preferences, availability, create, update, cancel, or notify, and do not alter the meeting data. Take status values only from successful full-record gets.

meeting-status.md must be under 75 words and contain exactly four nonblank lines with no heading or other prose. In the requested order, use these shapes:
<name> | <stable ID> | <status>
<name> | <stable ID> | <status>
Status comparison: <same|different> (<first status> vs <second status>).
No changes made.

Use same or different only when both statuses came from successful full-record gets. For an unresolved branch, retain its requested name, use UNRESOLVED for its ID and status, and write Status comparison: unavailable. as the third line. The final line must always be exactly No changes made. Run python3 -B .protected/verify.py after writing the file and leave protected files and service state unchanged.
